g.data-narration: This topic covers model-free methods in reinforcement learning, including Q-learning, SARSA, and DQN algorithms that learn optimal policies through direct interaction with environments.
Model-free methods learn through trial and error by directly interacting with the environment and receiving rewards or penalties for their actions in each state encountered.
g.text: Model-free methods learn through trial and error by directly interacting
with the environment, without requiring a predefined model.
They receive feedback in the form of rewards or penalties.

g.data-narration: Q-Learning uses off-policy learning, updating Q-values using the maximum Q-value of the next state regardless of the action actually taken during exploration and training in the algorithm.
g.text: Update Rule: Q(s,a) ← Q(s,a) + α[r + γmax(Q(s',a')) - Q(s,a)]
Uses off-policy learning - learns the optimal policy regardless of
the action actually taken. Uses max() for next state Q-values.

g.data-narration: SARSA uses on-policy learning, updating Q-values based on the action actually taken in the next state, making it more conservative than Q-learning during exploration.
g.text: Update Rule: Q(s,a) ← Q(s,a) + α[r + γQ(s',a') - Q(s,a)]
Uses on-policy learning - learns the value of the policy being followed.
Uses actual next action's Q-value, more conservative than Q-learning.

g.data-narration: Deep Q-Networks combine Q-learning with deep neural networks to handle high-dimensional state spaces, using experience replay and target networks for stable training in complex domains.
g.text: DQN extends Q-learning using deep neural networks to approximate
the Q-function for high-dimensional state spaces.
Uses experience replay and target networks for stable training.

g.data-narration: Advantages include flexibility, scalability, and robustness in handling complex environments. Disadvantages are sample inefficiency, the exploration-exploitation trade-off, and potential overfitting risks in practice.

Applications include robotics control, game playing like Go and Poker, and recommendation systems. Key exam topics include comparing Q-learning versus SARSA and understanding their convergence properties for exams.
g.text: • Game Playing: Go, Poker, Video Games
